title: Healthy No-Bake Millionaire’s Shortbread
summary: Millionaire's shortbread, but made with good stuff - sticky dates, creamy nut butter and a crunchy buckwheat base. What’s not to love?

method.description: Get the dates for the caramel into a bowl and cover with boiling water. Leave to soak for 20 mins, then drain.
For the base, add the buckwheat, dates, melted coconut oil and almond butter to a food processor. Blitz until you have a finely textured crumb.
Line a tray roughly 18cm x 24cm with baking paper, then press the base mixture in evenly using the back of a spoon. Firm it down well.

For the caramel, add the soaked dates, almond butter, water, maca powder (if using) and salt into a high-speed blender. Blitz until completely smooth then spoon the caramel over the base and spread it out evenly.

Melt the chocolate with the coconut oil on a low heat in the microwave or in heat-proof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water. Once melted, pour over the caramel layer and tip the tray side to side so the chocolate can flood the empty spaces.
Chill or freeze until set. If freezing, remove from the freezer and sit on the side for a couple of minutes before slicing. Use a warm knife to slice into bars for clean edges.

tips: Maca provides a caramelly flavour, but if you can't get it you can sub with caramel extract or leave it out entirely.
Store in the fridge for up to 1 week in a sealed container or freeze for up to 3 months and defrost for 5 mins before eating.
If you're using a smaller tray your bars will be thicker, just make smaller slices.

chefs.name: Rachel Pamart
chefs.summary: Rachel is the founder of Rawlala, a cold-pressed juicery based in East London. She came to clean, holistic eating by tuning into what made her feel most alive and now she channels that into expert, ingredient-led recipes designed to nourish, energise, and taste damn good.
